prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response:
Hi there! One common misconception many people have is the idea that multitasking helps to get more done. Research has consistently shown that multitasking, or switching between multiple tasks at once, actually decreases productivity and hampers our ability to complete tasks accurately and efficiently. Our brains are wired to focus on one thing at a time, and when we attempt to do multiple things at once, we might feel like we are accomplishing more, but in reality, our attention and the quality of our work suffer.

If you recognized yourself in this statement, consider trying out single-tasking or monotasking, where you focus your full attention on one task at a time. This can help you work more efficiently, retain information better, and complete tasks to a higher standard.
